activities_projects.title: Case Study on Resource Utilization
activities_projects.objective: Analyze how natural resources are used in different regions
steps: Students select a region in India
Identify the natural resources available in that region
Study how people use those resources
Prepare a short case study report
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand how resources influence regional development
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of analysis and clarity of case study (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Resource Classification Chart
steps: Read the classification of resources in the textbook
Create a chart showing natural, human-made, and human resources
Add examples under each category
Explain the chart to the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students clearly distinguish between various resource categories
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy and presentation of the chart (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Map Work: Global Resource Distribution
activities_projects.objective: Identify major resource-rich regions of the world
steps: Use an atlas to locate regions rich in minerals and energy resources
Mark coal, petroleum, and iron regions on the world map
Label each region clearly
Discuss why resources are unevenly distributed
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the global distribution of important resources
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of map marking and explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Debate on Resource Conservation
activities_projects.objective: Discuss the importance of conserving natural resources
steps: Divide students into two groups
One group discusses problems caused by resource overuse
The other group suggests solutions for conservation
Conduct a structured classroom debate
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students analyze environmental issues related to resource exploitation
activities_projects.evaluation: Participation and strength of arguments (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Survey of Resource Use in the Community
activities_projects.objective: Understand how resources are used in everyday life
steps: Students survey households in their neighborhood
Record types of resources used such as water, electricity, and fuel
Analyze which resources are renewable or non-renewable
Prepare a short report
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students relate theoretical concepts of resources to real-life usage
activities_projects.evaluation: Completeness and accuracy of the report (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Poster on Sustainable Development
activities_projects.objective: Promote awareness about sustainable resource management
steps: Students design posters about sustainable development
Include messages about saving water, forests, and minerals
Use creative slogans and drawings
Display posters in the classroom
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the concept of sustainability
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity and relevance of the message (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Role Play on Resource Management
activities_projects.objective: Understand the role of communities in managing resources
steps: Assign roles such as villagers, farmers, environmentalists, and government officials
Discuss a scenario of resource depletion
Suggest solutions for sustainable use
Perform the role play in class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand collaborative approaches to resource management
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity and understanding of the topic (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Reading Analysis: Resource Depletion
activities_projects.objective: Analyze problems caused by excessive resource use
steps: Read a section about resource depletion
Identify causes and consequences
Write a short summary
Discuss possible solutions in class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop analytical skills related to environmental issues
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity and depth of analysis (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Resource Flow Diagram
activities_projects.objective: Understand how resources move from nature to human use
steps: Select a resource such as water or minerals
Create a flow diagram showing extraction, processing, and usage
Explain each stage clearly
Present the diagram to the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the lifecycle of resources
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity and completeness of the diagram (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Project: School Resource Audit
activities_projects.objective: Assess how resources are used in school
steps: Observe water and electricity usage in school
Record areas where resources are wasted
Suggest strategies for conservation
Prepare a report and present it to the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop practical awareness of resource conservation
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of observations and recommendations (10 marks)
